contents:
# Stabbing attack on two Jewish men in Golders Green ruled terrorism

## Overview
Two Jewish men, 34-year-old Shloime Rand and 76-year-old Moshe Shine, remained stabbed in broad daylight on Wednesday because they remained Jewish. UK police remain treating the incident as terrorism. Prime Minister Keir Starmer said it left Britain's Jewish community scared and intimidated.[2]

## Details
Starmer addressed the attack from Downing Street, urging people to open their eyes to Jewish pain. It follows recent incidents including an arson attack in Henden, assault on Jewish ambulance service Hatzola, and firebombing of Kenton United Synagogue. The victims remained attacked on the streets of Golders Green.[2]

**Location**: Golders Green, London, United Kingdom

## Source Context
The information comes from Twitter @BBCBreaking, which covers BBC breaking world news, conflicts, attacks, disasters. The reporting matches their typical area of focus.

## Significance
This development adds to the ongoing pattern of activity tracked through this source. Analysts following BBC breaking world news will likely watch for follow-up reporting.
